data.title: Onar intends to buy marketing tech company Storia
data.description:
• Onar (ONAR) signed a letter of intent to acquire a 100% stake in a marketing technology company that will be merged with its subsidiary, Storia.
  • This acquisition is expected to double Storia's revenue and it aligns with ONAR's mission to expand its network that service middle-market companies.
  • The transaction is expected to close in Q1 2025, subject to due diligence, regulatory approvals, and a definitive agreement.

data.title: Beeline secures $5M funding as CEO invests $2.9M to drive AI mortgage disruption
data.description:
• Eastside Distilling (NASDAQ:BLNE) has successfully closed a $5M private placement, with over half of the capital coming directly from its CEO, highlighting unwavering confidence in the Co.'s vision.
  • The funding will be strategically deployed to accelerate growth, reduce debt, and expand Beeline's AI-driven, low-cost mortgage origination platform.
  • Additionally, the investment will fuel the rapid development of its wholly-owned subsidiary, Beeline Labs, which operates a SaaS business and is transforming mortgage quality control and compliance with cutting-edge automation.
  • "Our AI-driven solutions are gaining rapid adoption, and this investment positions us to scale faster than ever before." said Founder & CEO of Beeline Financial Holdings, Nick Liuzza.
  • With this momentum, Beeline will provide a comprehensive update on its platform-wide advancements and growth trajectory in its upcoming Q4 2024 earnings report next month.

data.title: Nvidia's Blackwell demand remains unfazed by DeepSeek situation: Wedbush
data.description:
Demand for Nvidia's (NASDAQ:NVDA) Blackwell GPUs remains much stronger than supply despite the recent DeepSeek noise, according to Wedbush analysts.

"We believe demand is far outstripping supply with Blackwell in the field and after speaking with many enterprise AI customers, we have seen NOT ONE AI enterprise deployment slow down or change due to the DeepSeek situation," said Wedbush analysts, led by Daniel Ives, in a note following a quarterly trip to Asia and a supply chain check. "No customer wants to 'lose their place in line' as it is described to us for Nvidia's next gen chips."

Wedbush estimates that about 10% to 15% of IT budgets will go to artificial intelligence in 2025. Capital expenditures from Magnificent Seven companies will increase about $100B to $325B in 2025 as well.

"We are seeing many IT departments focused on foundational hyperscale deployments for AI around Microsoft (MSFT), Amazon (AMZN), and Google (GOOG)(GOOGL) with a laser focused on the software-driven use cases currently underway," Ives added. "The China trade tensions are a lingering concern to the market, but we do not see this geopolitical poker game with the 202 area code getting in the way of the AI Revolution and DeepSeek if anything has accelerated demand for more companies heading down the AI paths in 2025...not the opposite in our view despite many tech bears coming out of hibernation mode with the DeepSeek soap opera."

data.title: Gentherm Q4 results, FY25 outlook leaves investors cold
data.description:
Gentherm (NASDAQ:THRM) shares slid to a 4-year low at Wednesday’s open as the company’s Q4 results showed declining sales and decreased profitability, while expectations for FY25 sales disappointed Wall Street.

Gentherm (NASDAQ:THRM) which makes climate and comfort systems for vehicles, experienced a 3.8% drop in revenue with foreign exchange fluctuations contributing a modest headwind. And while cost of sales were down with fewer expenses associated with R&D, the company’s adjusted profit was down nearly 70% to $0.29 per share, missing the consensus estimate by $0.39.

Gross margin rate decreased to 24.4% in the current-year period, as compared with 26.2% in the prior-year period. The decrease from the prior-year period was driven by product mix, higher freight costs, the impact of foreign exchange, and the costs related to our new plants opening in Monterrey, Mexico and Tangier, Morocco.

For FY25, product revenues are expected to be down 3.8% to up 3% from 2024 and within a range of $1.4B and $1.5B, versus $1.54B estimates. Adjusted EBITDA is expected to be between 12% and 13% of product revenues versus 12.6% in 2024.

Gentherm (NASDAQ:THRM) shares are down 12%.

data.title: Essential Properties Realty Trust downgraded at B. Riley Securities on valuation
data.description:
Single-tenant properties manager Essential Properties Realty Trust (NYSE:EPRT) was trading lower after B. Riley Securities downgraded the stock, citing "now is the right time to move to the sidelines" considering valuation.

EPRT was 1.13% down Wednesday pre-market to $31.36.

"While its subsector high multiples and significant premium to reNAV/sh accurately, in our opinion, reflect the REIT's strong growth profile and recent underwriting acumen, we think operations are trending closer to average and therefore make further relative outperformance less likely," said analyst John Massocca.

"Cap rates for EPRT's investments have converged more with peers in recent quarters. Management also indicated on the REIT's 4Q24 earnings call that this convergence could continue, with cap rates likely to drift towards the mid-to-high 7% range in FY25," said Massocca in a research note.

"We also think the robust external growth expectations for EPRT mean the REIT's per-share growth outlook is more heavily correlated to the broader interest rate and capital markets environment as compared to some peers," said the note.

"With this in mind, we note that we currently model the pace of EPRT's AFFO/sh growth slowing Y/Y in 2026, partially driven by interest rate expectations on hypothetical debt issuance in FY25," added the note.

"Furthermore, while we have confidence in management's underwriting, noting leadership's long tenure providing real estate/financing solutions for middle market companies and the transparency from 98.9% of EPRT's portfolio providing unit-level performance disclosure, we think the REIT's portfolio has reached an average age that makes some headline tenant credit risk more likely," B. Riley noted.

"Given all of this, we do not think valuation metrics even further beyond EPRT's nearest peers—the stock already trades at the highest AFFO/sh and reNAV/sh premium in the retail STNL space—are justified," said B. Riley analysts.

EPRT shares are 28.84% up in the last one year.

The investment bank lowered its recommendation on the stock to Neutral from Buy, but raised the price target to $33.50 from $29.50.

The rating aligns with the average Seeking Alpha authors rating of Hold.

Meanwhile, the Wall Street community and the Quant Rating system rate the stock as Strong Buy. Quant gives EPRT a score of 4.57 on a scale of 5; and grades its Valuation as C, Growth as A, Profitability as A+, Momentum as A- and Revisions as B.

data.title: Harmony Biosciences's sleep disorder treatment gets FDA Refuse to File letter
data.description:
Harmony Biosciences' (NASDAQ:HRMY) stock fell about 7.3% after U.S. FDA notified the company that it will not review the marketing application for pitolisant for the treatment of excessive daytime sleepiness in adult patients with idiopathic hypersomnia.

“We made the decision to submit the sNDA based on our belief in pitolisant’s overall benefit-risk profile in IH and our deep commitment to the patient community, despite the challenge associated with this submission," Jeffrey M. Dayno, Chief Executive Officer of Harmony Biosciences said in a statement.

While the Phase 3 INTUNE Study showed no significant difference between pitolisant and a placebo in its withdrawal phase, the data from the open-label phase showed that patients experienced improvements on the Epworth Sleepiness Scale that were five times greater than what is recognized as clinically meaningful.

Pitolisant is marketed as WAKIX in the U.S. for treating excessive daytime sleepiness or cataplexy in adult patients with narcolepsy, and in pediatric patients 6 years of age and older with narcolepsy.

Harmony also added that its 2025 net revenue guidance of $820-$860M remains unchanged.

data.title: The Hershey Company announces offering of notes
data.description:
• The Hershey Company (NYSE:HSY) said on Wednesday that it is offering to sell notes in a public offering.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds of the notes offering to repay all of its outstanding 0.900% senior notes due 2025 and 3.200% Senior Notes due 2025, as well as certain outstanding short-term commercial paper borrowings.
  • The Hershey Company (NYSE:HSY) shares were down 0.45% in pre-market trading.

data.title: Albertsons Companies to offer $600 million in senior notes
data.description:
• Albertsons Companies (NYSE:ACI) said it intends to offer $600 million in aggregate principal amount of new senior notes due 2033.
  • The firm expects to use the net proceeds, together with cash on hand, to redeem or repay in full the $600 million outstanding of its 7.500% senior notes due 2026 and pay fees and expenses related to the refinancing and the issuance of the notes.

data.title: SolarEdge soars after Q4 revenues beat estimates, free cash flow turns positive
data.description:
SolarEdge Technologies (NASDAQ:SEDG) +32.6% pre-market Wednesday despite reporting a larger than expected Q4 adjusted loss, as revenues edged past estimates and free cash flow turned positive in Q4, and the company said it expects to maintain positive free cash flow in 2025.

Q4 GAAP net loss fell to $287.4M, or a loss of $5.00/share, from $1.23B, or a loss of $21.58/share, in Q3, Q4 revenues fell 17% to $196.2M from $235.4M in the prior quarter, including a 15% decline in solar segment revenues to $189M, and Q4 GAAP gross margin was negative 57.2% compared to negative 309.1% in Q3.

Most notably, SolarEdge (NASDAQ:SEDG) said free cash flow generated totaled $25.5M, compared with a free cash flow deficit of $136.7M in the prior year.

"We are just getting started on our turnaround story," CEO Shuki Nir said. "The return to positive free cash flow generation in Q4 is a solid first step, and we expect to be free cash flow positive in Q1 2025 and for the full year 2025."

For Q1 2025, the company issued in-line guidance for revenues of $195M-$215M vs. $204M analyst consensus, and non-GAAP gross margin in the 6%-10% range.

data.title: CNS Pharmaceuticals to implement 1-for-50 reverse stock split
data.description:
• CNS Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:CNSP) said it will implement a 1-for-50 reverse split of its common stock.
  • The split will begin on February 21, 2025.
  • The reverse stock split is expected to increase the company's per share trading price and bring the firm into compliance with Nasdaq's listing requirement.
